subject: Anthropology
title: Naming and Power
description: A fast breakdown of Naming and Power for builders.

hook.text: Columbus called them Indians. They called themselves by their own names. Who gets to name whom is about power.

buildup.text: Naming practices reveal power relations. Colonizers renamed lands, peoples, and languages to assert control.

discovery.text: Personal naming varies culturally too. Some cultures keep names secret; others change names at life transitions.

twist.text: Reclaiming names is an act of resistance. When Indigenous peoples restore original place names, they restore identity.

climax.text: Names are never just labels. They carry history, power, and the right to define reality on your own terms.

punchline.text: To name something is to claim it.

quiz.question: Why is naming considered an act of power?
options: Naming defines reality and asserts control over identity
